repo.name: supatest-v0
repo.description: Supatest is a monorepo for an autonomous UI testing platform with a React frontend, Express API, browser recording extensions, BullMQ-based execution workers, Python Playwright runner, shared schemas, and supporting agent, email, CLI, and marketing packages.

clusters.label: Test Management Web Experience
clusters.summary: Delivers the authenticated React application for managing tests, plans, runs, dashboards, issues, environments, and the no-code editor.

clusters.label: Authenticated API Platform
clusters.summary: Exposes the core HTTP API, auth middleware, public endpoints, controllers, and request-level services used by the web app and integrations.

clusters.label: Test Execution Orchestration
clusters.summary: Queues, schedules, runs, and post-processes automated browser tests across regional workers and the Python Playwright runtime.

clusters.label: Recording And Realtime Browser Control
clusters.summary: Captures browser actions, screenshots, element-picking state, and realtime result streams through Chrome extensions and Socket.IO.

clusters.label: AI Test Generation And Healing
clusters.summary: Uses LLM services to generate DSL steps, Playwright Python code, chat-based test plans, readable logs, prompts, and failure analysis.

clusters.label: Data Contracts And Persistence
clusters.summary: Defines shared TypeScript/Zod contracts, Drizzle table models, database access, migrations, and cross-package constants.

clusters.label: Supporting Products And Integrations
clusters.summary: Provides the separate Agent API, CLI helpers, email templates, landing site, and lightweight proxy/tunnel support around the main platform.

nodes.label: Frontend App Shell
nodes.summary: Bootstraps Clerk auth, PostHog, React Query, global providers, layouts, and lazy-loaded routes for the authenticated product UI.

nodes.label: Test Management UI
nodes.summary: Implements pages and components for tests, folders, test plans, runs, issues, environments, and organization settings.

nodes.label: No-Code Test Editor
nodes.summary: Provides the visual test editor, step forms, live preview, recorder controls, run controls, and step-level AI assistance.

nodes.label: AI, Analytics, And Admin UI
nodes.summary: Renders AI chat, context hub, dashboards, run analytics, queue administration, and AI evaluation screens.

nodes.label: Shared UI Components
nodes.summary: Supplies reusable shadcn/Radix-based components, tables, forms, pickers, theme providers, and product-specific display widgets.

nodes.label: Express API Server
nodes.summary: Initializes Express, security middleware, logging, health checks, route registration, and graceful shutdown for the main API process.

nodes.label: Domain Controllers
nodes.summary: Implements authenticated REST endpoints for test cases, test plans, runs, grouped runs, scenarios, issues, folders, environments, variables, and attachments.

nodes.label: Auth And Organization Gates
nodes.summary: Enforces Clerk authentication, organization role checks, static auth for recording screenshots, super-admin access, and rate limiting.

nodes.label: Public API Surface
nodes.summary: Provides versioned API-key oriented endpoints for external clients to trigger test plan runs and manage environments.

nodes.label: BullMQ Queues
nodes.summary: Defines regional realtime and scheduled test queues plus common, AI usage, report, stuck-run, and cancellation job names.

nodes.label: Execution Workers
nodes.summary: Processes queued test jobs with Redis slot acquisition, regional worker selection, progress updates, retries, and failure handling.

nodes.label: Run Planning Services
nodes.summary: Creates grouped runs, test case run snapshots, scenario rows, runtime groups, and queue jobs for manual, scheduled, and editor-triggered execution.

nodes.label: Python Playwright Runner
nodes.summary: Hosts the generated pytest/Playwright runtime, realtime reporting utilities, browser state helpers, OpenTelemetry setup, and runner prompts.

nodes.label: Run Artifact Services
nodes.summary: Uploads and resolves screenshots, videos, traces, HTML snapshots, browser state, readable logs, and post-run status updates.

nodes.label: Socket.IO Coordination Server
nodes.summary: Authenticates and routes realtime connections from frontend editors, extensions, reporters, automation workers, and run workers.

nodes.label: Extension Background Runtime
nodes.summary: Maintains Chrome extension state, WebSocket connection, tab mapping, screenshot upload queue, and recorder lifecycle messages.

nodes.label: Extension Content Scripts
nodes.summary: Injects Playwright selector generation and event handlers into pages to capture clicks, inputs, scrolls, drags, frame paths, and element picker state.

nodes.label: Recorder Engine
nodes.summary: Packages a newer modular Chrome recording worker that buffers Playwright recorder actions, converts them to Supatest actions, and uploads screenshots.

nodes.label: DSL Step Generation
nodes.summary: Builds OpenAI prompts and Zod-constrained responses for converting page context and user instructions into valid Supatest DSL steps.

nodes.label: Playwright Code Generation
nodes.summary: Translates DSL steps, run configs, snippets, scenarios, and runtime context into executable Playwright Python code.

nodes.label: AI Chat And Context
nodes.summary: Manages AI conversations, context document enrichment, test-case context references, generated cases, and usage accounting.

nodes.label: Failure Analysis And Healing
nodes.summary: Categorizes execution failures, stores healing sessions and attempts, renders healing activity, and supports automated test repair flows.

nodes.label: Shared Schemas And Constants
nodes.summary: Exports cross-package Zod schemas, status constants, region constants, HTTP status codes, formatting utilities, and the shared step store.

nodes.label: Drizzle Domain Models
nodes.summary: Defines Drizzle tables and query helpers for organizations, users, tests, plans, runs, scenarios, browser sessions, AI usage, and issues.

nodes.label: Database Client And Migrations
nodes.summary: Configures PostgreSQL access, Drizzle migration execution, migration metadata, and one-off database scripts.

nodes.label: Environment And Runtime Config
nodes.summary: Centralizes API, frontend, extension, runner, Redis, OpenTelemetry, region, and third-party integration configuration.

nodes.label: Agent API
nodes.summary: Provides a separate FastAPI service for Supatest Agent v2 chat streaming, Clerk JWT auth, SQLModel persistence, and Claude Agent SDK integration placeholders.

nodes.label: CLI Utilities
nodes.summary: Contains command-line helpers for running plans and updating environment configuration outside the web UI.

nodes.label: Email Reports
nodes.summary: Renders React Email templates and helpers for sending test plan report emails from backend jobs.

nodes.label: Landing Site And Docs
nodes.summary: Hosts the public marketing site, guide pages, docs, pricing, FAQ, testimonials, and content assets.

nodes.label: Proxy And Tunnel Helpers
nodes.summary: Includes lightweight packages and documentation for proxy and tunnel-manager support around browser execution environments.

steps.description: A user starts a test case or grouped run from the React UI.
The API validates the request, loads test case and run configuration data, and creates run records.
Run services snapshot steps, expand snippets and scenarios, create grouped runtime metadata, and prepare jobs.
BullMQ enqueues regional realtime or scheduled run jobs backed by Redis.
Workers acquire concurrency slots, update progress, and generate execution data when needed.
The worker converts DSL steps and runtime context into Playwright Python code.
Generated pytest code executes against Playwright browsers in the Python runner.
Post-processing stores logs, videos, screenshots, traces, snapshots, and final statuses.
The no-code editor starts or stops recording for a selected test case.
Socket.IO coordinates the editor and extension sessions for recording messages.
The extension tracks recording state, tab changes, screenshots, and buffered actions.
Content scripts capture DOM events, generate locators, and report actions from frames.
Recording handlers forward action and screenshot events back to the server-side session.
The API stores or updates the test case steps and screenshot references.
The editor renders the new steps for review, editing, and execution.
A user submits AI chat or generation instructions with optional context documents and test references.
The API aggregates conversation history, context documents, and referenced test cases.
Prompt services produce validated DSL steps from instructions, HTML context, snippets, and schemas.
Zod schemas and shared constants constrain the generated test step shape.
Generated steps or cases are persisted through test case endpoints and models.
The editor displays AI-generated steps in the test-building workflow.

sections.title: Overview
sections.body_markdown: Supatest is organized as a pnpm monorepo around a React product UI, an Express API, shared TypeScript contracts, Chrome recording extensions, BullMQ workers, and a Python Playwright runner. The central product loop is authoring UI test steps, recording browser interactions, generating or editing steps with AI, executing tests through regional queues, and collecting artifacts and analytics.

sections.title: Architecture
sections.body_markdown: The frontend calls the authenticated Express API using Clerk-issued bearer tokens and imports shared schemas plus reusable UI components. The API owns routing, domain orchestration, Drizzle-backed persistence, queue creation, AI service calls, and public API endpoints. Long-running work is pushed into BullMQ queues and processed by worker processes that generate code, invoke the Python runner, and update database state.

sections.title: Key Flows
sections.body_markdown: The most important flows are manual or scheduled test execution, browser recording into editable DSL steps, and AI-assisted authoring. Realtime coordination uses the Socket.IO server for editor, extension, reporter, and run-worker connections, while execution state and final artifacts are persisted through the API models and storage upload services.

sections.title: Entry Points
sections.body_markdown: Primary runtime entry points include `frontend/src/main.tsx` for the web app, `api/src/server.ts` for the HTTP API, `api/src/ws-server/server.ts` for realtime sockets, `api/src/workers/test-run.worker.ts` for test execution workers, `extension/src/background.ts` and `extension/src/content/index.ts` for the Chrome extension, and `agent/app/main.py` for the separate Agent API.

sections.title: Data Model
sections.body_markdown: Shared schemas in `shared/src/schemas` define API contracts for tests, runs, plans, scenarios, environments, browser sessions, issues, and AI usage. Drizzle model files under `api/src/models` define the persistence layer and are re-exported through `api/src/models/schema.ts` for migration generation.

sections.title: Getting Started
sections.body_markdown: The root README describes building `shared`, installing dependencies with pnpm, running API migrations from `api`, starting `frontend`, `api`, and the runner-related services, and using the extension in Chrome debug mode. For local architecture exploration, start with the shared schemas, API route registration, frontend route tree, queue definitions, and worker execution path.

pullRequests.title: Bootstrap Express API server and Drizzle persistence layer
pullRequests.author: arjun.menon
pullRequests.summary: Stands up the initial Express app with security middleware, health checks, and graceful shutdown, plus the first Drizzle models for organizations, users, and test cases.

pullRequests.title: Add shared Zod contracts and constants package
pullRequests.author: lena.fischer
pullRequests.summary: Introduces the shared schemas package consumed by both the API and frontend, including test-step, test-case, and run schemas plus region and status constants.

pullRequests.title: Scaffold authenticated React app shell with Clerk and React Query
pullRequests.author: priya.nair
pullRequests.summary: Builds the frontend app shell with Clerk auth guard, React Query provider, global layouts, and lazy-loaded routes, wiring it to the API utils client.

pullRequests.title: Implement domain controllers and auth/org middleware
pullRequests.author: arjun.menon
pullRequests.summary: Adds REST controllers for test cases, plans, runs, issues, and environments behind Clerk auth and organization role gates with rate limiting.

pullRequests.title: Add BullMQ execution queues and regional workers
pullRequests.author: diego.santos
pullRequests.summary: Defines regional realtime and scheduled queues backed by Redis and the worker processes that acquire concurrency slots, track progress, and handle retries.

pullRequests.title: Wire Python Playwright runner with realtime reporting
pullRequests.author: diego.santos
pullRequests.summary: Adds the pytest/Playwright runtime, realtime reporter, browser-state helpers, and artifact upload services for screenshots, videos, and traces.

pullRequests.title: Chrome extension recording pipeline and Socket.IO server
pullRequests.author: marek.kowalski
pullRequests.summary: Introduces the Socket.IO coordination server, extension background runtime, and content scripts that capture DOM events and generate Playwright locators.

pullRequests.title: AI DSL step generation and Playwright code generation
pullRequests.author: lena.fischer
pullRequests.summary: Adds OpenAI-backed DSL step generation with Zod-constrained responses and the codegen path that turns DSL steps into executable Playwright Python.

pullRequests.title: Modular recorder engine to replace legacy content capture
pullRequests.author: marek.kowalski
pullRequests.summary: Ships a newer modular recording worker that buffers Playwright recorder actions, converts them to Supatest actions, and uploads screenshots asynchronously.

pullRequests.title: AI chat conversations, context hub, and usage accounting
pullRequests.author: priya.nair
pullRequests.summary: Adds AI conversations, context-document enrichment, and the context hub UI, with token-usage accounting surfaced on the analytics dashboard.

pullRequests.title: Automated failure analysis and self-healing test repair
pullRequests.author: lena.fischer
pullRequests.summary: Categorizes execution failures, persists healing sessions and attempts, and renders healing activity so flaky tests can be repaired automatically.

pullRequests.title: Run analytics dashboard refresh and queue admin metrics
pullRequests.author: priya.nair
pullRequests.summary: Reworks the dashboard overview with run-trend charts and live queue-depth metrics, plus a refreshed AI step-eval screen for the admin area.

dbTables.name: organizations
dbTables.summary: Top-level tenant; owns all projects, users, and billing context.

dbTables.name: users
dbTables.summary: Authenticated members synced from Clerk, scoped to an organization.

dbTables.name: projects
dbTables.summary: A workspace grouping test cases, plans, and environments.

dbTables.name: test_cases
dbTables.summary: An individual UI test composed of ordered DSL steps.

dbTables.name: test_steps
dbTables.summary: Ordered DSL steps belonging to a test case, including selectors and actions.

dbTables.name: test_plans
dbTables.summary: A named collection of test cases executed together on a schedule or on demand.

dbTables.name: environments
dbTables.summary: Target base URL plus variables used when executing a plan or case.

dbTables.name: grouped_runs
dbTables.summary: A batch execution of a test plan that fans out into individual case runs.

dbTables.name: test_case_runs
dbTables.summary: Execution record for a single test case within a grouped run.

dbTables.name: run_artifacts
dbTables.summary: Screenshots, videos, traces, and HTML snapshots produced by a case run.

dbTables.name: recordings
dbTables.summary: Captured browser recording session converted into Supatest actions.

dbTables.name: api_keys
dbTables.summary: Hashed API keys used by external clients to trigger plan runs.

dbTables.name: ai_generations
dbTables.summary: Record of an AI step/code generation request with token usage and cost.

dbTables.name: healing_sessions
dbTables.summary: A self-healing attempt session opened after a test case run fails.

functions.name: createTestCaseHandler
functions.summary: POST /test-cases controller; validates the body and delegates to the test-case service.

functions.name: listTestCasesHandler
functions.summary: GET /test-cases controller; resolves project scope then queries cases.

functions.name: startGroupedRunHandler
functions.summary: POST /runs controller; kicks off a grouped run for a plan or set of cases.

functions.name: createTestCase
functions.summary: Service that persists a new test case and its initial steps.

functions.name: runTestCase
functions.summary: Snapshots steps and enqueues a single test-case run job.

functions.name: runTestPlan
functions.summary: Expands a plan into a grouped run and fans out per-case jobs.

functions.name: runGroupedRun
functions.summary: Creates runtime groups and enqueues regional jobs for a grouped run.

functions.name: insertTestCase
functions.summary: Drizzle insert into test_cases.

functions.name: queryTestCases
functions.summary: Drizzle select of test cases for a project.

functions.name: insertGroupedRun
functions.summary: Drizzle insert into grouped_runs.

functions.name: insertTestCaseRun
functions.summary: Drizzle insert into test_case_runs.

functions.name: updateRunStatus
functions.summary: Drizzle update of a test_case_run status and timing.

functions.name: enqueueRunJob
functions.summary: Adds a run-test job to the regional BullMQ queue.

functions.name: acquireConcurrencySlot
functions.summary: Acquires a Redis-backed concurrency slot for a region.

functions.name: processRunJob
functions.summary: Worker entry point that drives a single test-case run end to end.

functions.name: reportProgress
functions.summary: Emits run progress updates over the worker channel.

functions.name: generateDslSteps
functions.summary: Builds the OpenAI prompt and parses a Zod-validated DSL step list.

functions.name: buildPromptBlocks
functions.summary: Assembles HTML context, snippets, and schema blocks for the prompt.

functions.name: validateSteps
functions.summary: Validates raw model output against the shared test-step schema.

functions.name: generatePlaywrightPyCode
functions.summary: Turns DSL steps and a run config into executable Playwright Python.

functions.name: renderReadableBody
functions.summary: Produces a human-readable narration of the generated test body.

functions.name: runPytestSession
functions.summary: Executes generated pytest/Playwright code and streams realtime results.

functions.name: emitRealtimeEvent
functions.summary: Sends step-level realtime events from the runner back to the server.

functions.name: handleTestRunPostProcessing
functions.summary: Uploads artifacts and writes the final run status after execution.

functions.name: uploadScreenshots
functions.summary: Uploads screenshots to storage and returns their URLs.

functions.name: handleRecordingEvent
functions.summary: Routes recording action/screenshot events to the active session.

functions.name: authenticateSocket
functions.summary: Validates a Clerk token on the realtime connection.

functions.name: bufferRecorderAction
functions.summary: Buffers a Playwright recorder action before conversion.

functions.name: toSupatestAction
functions.summary: Converts a raw recorder action into a Supatest DSL action.

functions.name: generateLocators
functions.summary: Generates ranked Playwright selectors for a captured element.

functions.name: sendWsMessage
functions.summary: Sends a buffered message over the extension WebSocket connection.

functions.name: generateConversationalResponse
functions.summary: Aggregates conversation history and context docs to answer an AI chat turn.

functions.name: recordAiUsage
functions.summary: Persists token usage and cost for an AI generation.

functions.name: analyzeFailure
functions.summary: Categorizes a failed run and opens a healing session if recoverable.

functions.name: attemptHeal
functions.summary: Regenerates broken steps and records a healing attempt.

functions.name: useTestCases
functions.summary: React Query hook that fetches test cases for the management UI.

functions.name: apiGet
functions.summary: Authenticated fetch wrapper used by frontend data hooks.

functions.name: triggerPublicPlanRun
functions.summary: Public v1 endpoint that triggers a plan run via API key.

services.name: Web App
services.summary: React single-page app for test management, the no-code editor, and the AI dashboard.

services.name: API Server
services.summary: Express API exposing domain controllers, auth middleware, AI services, and the public API.

services.name: WebSocket Server
services.summary: Realtime WebSocket server that streams recorder events between the extension and the editor.

services.name: Execution Workers
services.summary: BullMQ workers that consume run jobs, drive the Python runner, and persist artifacts.

services.name: Python Runner
services.summary: Python Playwright runner that executes generated test steps in a real browser.

services.name: Recorder Extension
services.summary: Browser extension that records user interactions and streams them to the WebSocket server.

services.name: Agent Service
services.summary: Standalone agent service backing AI-driven automation flows.

services.name: Email Reports
services.summary: Service that renders and delivers test run report emails.

services.name: Landing & Docs
services.summary: Marketing landing site and product documentation.

services.name: Proxy Tunnel
services.summary: Proxy/tunnel that exposes local app under test to the execution environment.

services.name: CLI
services.summary: Command-line interface for triggering runs and managing projects.

serviceLinks.summary: Frontend calls REST API for test management and AI features.
No-code editor subscribes to live recorder events over WebSocket.
Extension background streams recorded steps to the WebSocket server.
API enqueues run jobs onto BullMQ for the workers.
Workers invoke the Python Playwright runner to execute steps.
Workers emit run-complete events that trigger report emails.
API delegates agentic automation tasks to the agent service.
CLI drives runs and project management through the API.
Runner reaches the app under test through the proxy tunnel.
Landing site submits signups and contact requests to the API.
